jobs.description:
Jobright is a next-generation AI job search platform built to make career navigation faster, smarter, and more personal. They are looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw data into insights that shape how our AI agents learn and improve.

Why Join Us

Work on AI systems that are actively changing how people find careers
Your analysis drives real product decisions, not dashboards no one reads

Responsibilities

Surface data-driven insights that inform how our AI agents are tuned, evaluated, and improved over time
Build reports and visualizations that make findings clear for both product teams and external stakeholders
Turn around data requests quickly, translating analytical outputs into accessible narratives

Qualifications

Required

Recent grad or early-career professional with 0 to 2 years of experience in data analysis or a related technical field
Strong communicator able to present trends clearly to technical and non-technical audiences
Working understanding of statistics and the basics of how AI and machine learning systems work
Must be based in and authorized to work in the United States

Preferred

Internship or project experience in data analysis, research, or a support role at a tech or AI-focused organization
Hands-on skills in SQL, Python or R, and basic data modeling

jobs.title: Sr. Health & Benefits Data Analyst
jobs.description:
SGS is the world's leading Testing, Inspection and Certification company. We operate a network of over 2,700 laboratories and business facilities across 119 countries, supported by a team of 99,250 dedicated professionals. With over 145 years of service excellence, we combine the precision and accuracy that define Swiss companies to help organizations achieve the highest standards of quality, safety and compliance.

Our brand promise, when you need to be sure , underscores our commitment to reliability, integrity and trust — enabling businesses to thrive with confidence. We proudly deliver our expert services through the SGS name and trusted specialized brands, including Brightsight, Bluesign, Maine Pointe and Nutrasource.

SGS is publicly traded on the SIX Swiss Exchange under the ticker symbol SGSN (ISIN CH0002497458, Reuters SGSN.S, Bloomberg SGSN:SW).

Job Description

The Sr. Health & Benefits Data Analyst turns complex health and benefits data into clear, actionable insights. This role uses advanced data analytics, multivariate analyses, dashboards, benchmarking, and AI-driven analysis to show how effective programs deliver measurable results, helping position the organization as a leader in benefit analytics. We are looking to hire at least 2 positions.

Job functions:

Data Analysis & Insights

Analyze data related to benefits, including medical claims, pharmacy claims, wellbeing programs, ergonomics, workers’ compensation, disability, flexible work schedule, paid time off, and other benefits. Identify trends, correlations, and leading indicators linking health and wellbeing programs, initiatives, and design to reductions in claims, accidents, and lost-time incidents.
Support descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analyses across benefits programs (e.g., mental health, fertility, medical, ergonomics, occupational health and safety (OHS)).
Enable ongoing evaluation of benefit suppliers and point solutions, including program effectiveness, utilization, outcomes, and cost impact.
Build predictive models to forecast risk exposure, claim frequency/severity, and potential impact of preventive interventions.
Design and deploy AI-driven models (machine learning, NLP, anomaly detection) to uncover hidden patterns in health, safety, and benefits data.
Translate business and research questions into analytic requirements and ensure outputs are accurate, interpretable, and decision‑ready.
Review analytic outputs for quality, validity, and appropriate interpretation before sharing with stakeholders. Provide training and support to benefits managers for data interpretation and validity.
Coordinate with leaders, actuary, and benefit managers to develop and automate recurring reports to ensure timely and scalable insights.

Impact Measurement & ROI Demonstration

Quantify the financial and human impact of safety and wellbeing initiatives.
Develop frameworks to measure return-on-investment (ROI) and value-on-investment (VOI) for health, benefits, and safety programs.
Produce executive-ready narratives that clearly link prevention, wellbeing, and safety investments to business outcomes.
Develop standardized key performance indicators (KPIs) and indices (e.g., incident/utilization rates, claim costs, wellbeing return-on-investment (ROI) metrics) to enable consistent comparisons across industries and geographies.
Use benchmarking insights to translate clear recommendations for program design, investment prioritization, and risk mitigation.

Stakeholder Collaboration

Collaborate closely with a third party analytics vendor and internal health and benefits program managers to design, streamline, and maintain dashboards that support data driven decision-making. Ensure consistent definitions, data quality, and visualization standards across reporting products while translating program needs into clear analytical requirements and insights
Support leadership with data driven insights for strategic decisions related to benefits design, wellbeing initiatives, and safety programs.
Act as a subject matter expert in benefit analytics.
Help manage analytic demand to avoid backlogs, maintain momentum, and ensure sustained analytic capacity.

Governance, Ethics, & Compliance

Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and appropriate use of sensitive health and employee data.
Comply with all relevant data privacy, security, and regulatory requirements.
Support fiduciary responsibilities related to benefits decision‑making by ensuring analyses are rigorous, unbiased, and well‑documented.

Key Deliverables

Executive dashboards and reports linking health, wellbeing, and OHS programs to measurable outcomes.
Industry benchmarking reports with clear performance positioning.
Predictive risk and claims models.
ROI, evaluations, and impact assessments for safety, health, and benefits initiatives.
Data-driven recommendations for continuous improvement.

Qualifications

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Economics, Public Health, Healthcare Economics, Occupational Health, Engineering, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in data analytics, preferably within public health, insurance, benefits, risk management, or occupational health in a large, complex organization.
Strong experience with data analysis tools (e.g., Python, R, SQL, etc) and experience designing and establishing dashboards from raw data using tools such as Power BI
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain complex analyses to non‑technical audiences.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience working with health insurance, wellbeing, disability, or benefits programs or datasets, especially for large employers.
Experience demonstrating program evaluations, outcome measurements, and ROI of health, wellbeing, or safety programs.
Experience working as a liaison between groups to help reach decisions
PhD or PhDc in Data Science, Statistics, Economics, Public Health, Occupational Health, Engineering, or a related field.
Experience supporting executive‑level or senior leadership decision‑making.
Experience with AI/ML techniques for analysis or to generate program insights

Compensation

The expected salary range for this position is $115,000 – $175,000 annually. This range represents the minimum and maximum base salary we reasonably expect to pay for this role. Actual compensation within the range will depend on skills, experience, and qualifications.

Our Benefits

We care about your total well-being and will support you with the following, subject to your location and role.

Health:
Medical, dental and vision insurance, life insurance, employee assistance programs.
Wealth:
In addition to base pay, we offer 401(k) with company match (immediate vesting upon enrollment).
Happiness:
Professional Growth:
Online training courses, virtual and classroom development experiences, tuition reimbursement program
Work-Life Balance:
Paid-time off and family leave

In compliance with applicable state and local pay transparency laws, we provide clear and equitable compensation information for all applicants.

Additional Information

SGS is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and as such we recruit, hire, train, and promote persons in all job classifications without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, age, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetics, status as a protected veteran, or any other characteristics protected by law.

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily with or without reasonable accommodations. The requirements listed above are representative of the knowledge, skills, and/or abilities required.

This job description should not be construed as an exhaustive statement of duties, responsibilities or requirements, but a general description of the job. Nothing contained herein restricts the company’s rights to assign or reassign duties and responsibilities to this job at any time.

If you are applying for a position within the United States and you have difficulty completing the on-line employment application because of a disability, please call 201-508-3149 for assistance and leave a message. You will receive a call back. Please note, this phone number is not for general employment information, but is only for individuals who are experiencing difficulty applying for a position due to a disability.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Jobright is a next-generation AI job search platform built to make career navigation faster, smarter, and more personal. They are looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw data into actionable intelligence that shapes how our agents learn, adapt, and perform.

Why Join Us

Work directly on AI systems that are actively changing how people find careers
Your analysis will influence real product decisions, not just sit in dashboards
Collaborate across product, engineering, and client teams in a high-ownership environment
Be part of a team that takes data seriously as a driver of agent improvement

Responsibilities

Embed directly into client-facing and internal workflows, ensuring data-driven insights are woven into the decision-making processes of our AI agents
Design and deliver custom data reports and visualizations that surface clear, expert-level findings for both product teams and external stakeholders
Field data requests with speed and precision, translating complex analytical outputs into accessible, well-communicated narratives
Collaborate regularly with your manager and cross-functional teammates to continuously refine data models and track agent performance over time

Qualifications

Required

Recent grad or early-career professional with 0–2 years of experience in data analysis or a related technical field
Strong communicator with the ability to present data trends clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Solid grounding in statistics, analytical methodologies, and the underlying logic of AI and machine learning systems
Must be based in and authorized to work in the United States; occasional travel for team summits may be required

Preferred

Prior internship or project experience in a data analyst, research, or support role within a tech or AI-focused organization
Demonstrated ability to extract practical, digestible insights from large and complex datasets
Technical proficiency in Python or R, data modeling concepts, and SQL-based database management

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
As a global medtech company, we are driven by our Vision of changing the trajectory of lives for a new day and our Mission to create ingenious solutions that ignite patient turnarounds. Our relentless commitment to patients and strong legacy of innovation in healthcare are the foundation of our future. If you're looking for a new chance, a new beginning, a new trajectory, LivaNova is where your talent can truly thrive. Join our talented team members worldwide to become a pioneer of tomorrow—because at LivaNova, we don’t just treat conditions — we aspire to alter the course of lives.

The Data Analyst will provide data analysis, reporting, and visualization support to the Statistics & Data Science team across post-market surveillance, manufacturing, R&D, and clinical data sources. The Data Analyst will accomplish this by developing efficient, repeatable analytical solutions by automating routine reports, maintaining dashboards, and improving data workflows in collaboration with statisticians and business stakeholders. Additionally, they will translate stakeholder needs into practical data deliverables, with guidance, while ensuring outputs are accurate, validated, documented, and aligned with internal procedures.

Essential Job Functions

Generate routine and ad hoc descriptive analyses, summaries, tables, graphs, and reports to support operational, quality, product, and clinical decision-making.
Develop, automate, and maintain recurring reports and analytical workflows using programming tools and reproducible code to improve efficiency, consistency, and timeliness.
Build, enhance, and maintain dashboards and other data visualization tools that enable stakeholders to monitor key metrics and trends.
Transform stakeholder questions into clear analytical requirements by clarifying objectives, defining deliverables, and confirming timelines, assumptions, and data needs.
Partner with statisticians and subject matter experts to determine when a request is appropriate for a data analyst solution versus when deeper methodological or statistical expertise is required.
Perform data extraction, data cleaning, data preparation, quality checks, and documentation activities to support reliable analyses and reporting.
Support validation and code review activities for analytical programs and reports in accordance with internal procedures, documentation practices, and quality expectations.
Identify opportunities to streamline existing reporting processes, standardize recurring deliverables, and implement practical process improvements that reduce manual effort.
Communicate results clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders through effective written, visual, and verbal summaries, and manage multiple requests/projects with appropriate organization and follow-through.

Minimum Requirements

0-3 years of professional experience in a comparable role supporting data analytics, preferably within a medical device, pharmaceutical company, or CRO.
Bachelor’s degree with excellent track record in mathematics, statistics, data science, computer science, business analytics or equivalent (master’s preferable).
Strong programming skills in R or Python, with experience in both. Advantageous to be familiar with SQL, Power BI, SAS, VBA, JavaScript.
Understanding of mathematical and statistical principles.
Willingness and ability to develop knowledge of device functionality.
Ability to effectively collaborate and communicate cross-functionally with engineers and clinical or medical staff.

Travel Requirements

Occasional travel may be required.

Pay Transparency

A reasonable estimate of the annual base salary for this position is $70,000 - $100,000 plus discretionary annual bonus. Pay ranges may vary by location and will be awarded based on experience.

Employee Benefits Include

Health benefits – Medical, Dental, Vision
Personal and Vacation Time
Retirement & Savings Plan (401K)
Employee Stock Purchase Plan
Training & Education Assistance
Bonus Referral Program
Service Awards
Employee Recognition Program
Flexible Work Schedules

Welcome to impact. Welcome to innovation. Welcome to your new life.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
About The Company

My3Tech is a leading technology solutions provider dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable IT services to a diverse range of clients. With a focus on cutting-edge technology and customer-centric approaches, we strive to empower businesses by providing tailored solutions that enhance efficiency, security, and growth. Our team of highly skilled professionals is committed to maintaining the highest standards of quality and excellence, ensuring that our clients stay ahead in an ever-evolving digital landscape.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented IT Project Manager to join our dynamic team at My3Tech. In this role, you will be responsible for planning, executing, and overseeing various technology projects from initiation to completion. The ideal candidate will possess excellent leadership skills, a strong technical background, and the ability to coordinate cross-functional teams effectively. You will serve as the primary point of contact for clients and internal stakeholders, ensuring project deliverables are met on time, within scope, and within budget. This position offers an exciting opportunity to work on innovative projects that make a tangible impact on our clients' businesses.

Qualifications

The ideal candidate should have a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. A minimum of 5 years of experience in project management within the IT industry is required. Certifications such as PMP, PRINCE2, or Agile Scrum are highly desirable. Strong understanding of software development lifecycle, infrastructure, and cybersecurity is essential. Excellent communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills are a must. Experience working with cross-functional teams and managing multiple projects simultaneously is preferred. A proactive attitude and the ability to adapt to changing priorities are key attributes for success in this role.

Responsibilities

Develop comprehensive project plans, including scope, objectives, timelines, and resource allocation to ensure successful project delivery.

Coordinate internal resources and third-party vendors for the flawless execution of projects.

Monitor project progress and make adjustments as needed to ensure timely completion.

Communicate project status, issues, and risks effectively to stakeholders and senior management.

Ensure all projects adhere to quality standards and compliance requirements.

Identify and mitigate potential project risks through proactive planning and problem resolution.

Facilitate regular project meetings to track progress and address any challenges.

Maintain comprehensive documentation related to project activities, deliverables, and lessons learned.

Support continuous improvement initiatives by analyzing project outcomes and implementing best practices.

Assist in resource planning and capacity management to optimize project execution.

Benefits

My3Tech offers a competitive salary package aligned with industry standards, along with performance-based incentives. We provide comprehensive health insurance coverage, including medical, dental, and vision plans, to support the well-being of our employees and their families. Our company also offers generous paid time off, including holidays, vacation, and personal days, to promote work-life balance. Employees have access to ongoing professional development opportunities, including training programs, certifications, and workshops, to foster career growth. Additionally, we promote a collaborative and inclusive work environment that values diversity, innovation, and teamwork. Our employees enjoy flexible working arrangements to accommodate their personal and professional needs, along with various employee engagement activities throughout the year.

Equal Opportunity

My3Tech is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe in fostering a diverse workforce that reflects the communities we serve and are dedicated to providing equal employment opportunities to all qualified candidates. Our commitment to diversity and inclusion is integral to our company culture and success.

jobs.title: Data Analyst - Procurement
jobs.description:
Job description:

Basic Function
Supports the procurement organization’s goals and objectives by leveraging advanced analytical techniques and tools to transform raw data into strategic insights that enhance decision-making and drive operational success. Manage complex datasets, develop sophisticated analytics solutions, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, optimize processes, and implement impactful improvements. Ensures data accuracy and integrity while communicating findings effectively to diverse stakeholders, influencing organizational strategies and fostering data-driven innovation. Job Duties
ESSENTIAL JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: (functions considered essential as defined by ADA)
Data Collection & Management:
Gather, organize, and manage complex datasets from various internal and external sources.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity through rigorous audits, validation, and quality control measures.
Design and maintain advanced data models to support seamless data processing and automated reporting.

Data Analysis & Insights:
Conduct sophisticated analyses using statistical, predictive, and machine learning techniques to solve complex problems and drive business value.
Identify trends, correlations, and opportunities through deep-dive data exploration and advanced modeling.
Develop actionable recommendations to inform strategies and improve business outcomes.

Reporting & Visualization:
Design and implement dynamic dashboards, reports, and visualizations to clearly communicate complex insights.
Tailor presentations to diverse audiences, ensuring both technical and non-technical stakeholders can understand and utilize insights effectively.
Provide proactive and timely responses to ad-hoc reporting needs.

Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement:
Partner with cross-functional teams and leadership to understand their data needs and provide expert-level analytical support.
Act as a key liaison between business units and technical teams to ensure alignment on project goals, metrics, and deliverables.
Mentor and train team members on data tools, systems, and best practices to elevate organizational analytics capabilities.

Process Improvement & Innovation:
Identify and lead initiatives to improve data collection, storage, and analysis.
Develop strategies to automate workflows, enhance efficiency, and support scalable growth.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects that leverage data to innovate and create new business opportunities.

Data Quality & Governance:
Establish and uphold best practices for data governance, ensuring compliance with organizational policies and standards.
Oversee data cleansing, enrichment, and transformation processes to ensure high-quality, reliable datasets.
Advocate for and support policies that promote data integrity and effective data management.

Leadership & Special Projects:
Drive high-impact projects that align with organizational goals and deliver measurable outcomes.
Provide guidance based on data-driven insights to influence decision-making at the leadership level.
Foster a culture of continuous improvement and innovation.
Lead or contribute to special assignments or organizational initiatives as required.

Special assignments or tasks assigned to the employee by their supervisor, as determined from time to time in their sole and complete discretion.
Experience
A minimum of 0- 2 years related experience is required.

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:
Refinery experience and/or experience in procurement, accounting, and inventory management are preferred.
Education Level
A minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree, in a technical or business related field. Each year of experience over five in procurement or inventory control may be substituted for one year of education (nine years total experience required w/o education).
Required Skills
Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize analyze and disseminate significant quantities of information with attention to detail and accuracy; knowledge of the procurement / contracting business and ability to apply this knowledge in providing supporting analytics and reporting; Advanced reading and writing skills; strong background and extensive knowledge of Excel (focus on Power Query/Power Pivot for data transformations); Power BI, SAP tables and QuickViewer query builder, SAP scripting and Microsoft VBA to automate routine manual tasks; familiarity with SQL; proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite; experience with requirements gathering and process documentation; ability to analyze and reconcile data across multiple data sets and systems, interpret data into insights, and develop actionable recommendations; experience with manual bulk data processing using reports/queries and uploads; experience in data analysis methods and using a variety of tools to develop visualizations; business process analysis and change management; extracting data from tables, building queries in SAP; preparing and using large data sets to create dashboards.;strong communication skills to present data-driven insights to senior leadership.
Supervisory/Managerial Responsibility
May have direct reports (contractor and/or HF Sinclair staff).
Work Conditions
Office based with occasional refinery work and with up to 25% travel required by land or air. May include petroleum refinery, warehouse/plant environment, and/or out-of-doors environment.
Benefits
HF Sinclair offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support the well-being of our employees and their families. Our benefits include, but are not limited to, the following: Medical Insurance
Vision Insurance
Dental Insurance
Paid Time-Off
401(k) Retirement Plan with match
Educational Reimbursement
Parental Bonding Time
Employee Discounts

We are committed to fostering a supportive and inclusive work environment, ensuring our employees have the resources needed to thrive professionally and personally. Benefit eligibility is governed by official plan documents, for more details visit Total Rewards. Physical Requirements
Job conditions require, walking, sitting, twisting, stooping, crouching, kneeling, talking or hearing, making precise hand and finger movements, and perceiving color differences, the ability to wear personal protective equipment (beards not permitted), and the ability to operate and drive all assigned company vehicles at company standard insurance rates is essential, valid State driver’s license and proof of insurance required.  Job conditions may require reaching or grasping, lifting and/or carrying up to 20lbs, pushing and/or pulling up to 20 lbs, and climbing up to 15ft. 
Our One HF Sinclair Culture:
At HF Sinclair, we are united through our One HF Sinclair Culture, which is underpinned by our five core values of Safety, Integrity, Teamwork, Ownership and Inclusion. Developed to empower our people, our five core cultural values are at the heart of everything we do and extend to how we engage our stakeholders. These values influence our decisions, shape our behaviors and keep us connected across the entire organization. We maintain a true Safety culture for our employees, communities, environments and customers. Our goal is to make sure everyone returns home safely each day. We have a long-standing commitment to Integrity and ethical behavior and do what is right for our employees, investors, communities and the environment. We encourage employees to Step Up and Stand Out by championing a culture of Teamwork and Ownership. We foster a culture of Inclusion by encouraging diversity of experiences, viewpoints and backgrounds. What makes each of us different, together makes us stronger. About HF Sinclair Corporation
HF Sinclair Corporation, headquartered in Dallas, Texas, is an independent energy company that produces and markets high-value light products such as gasoline, diesel fuel, jet fuel, renewable diesel and other specialty products. HF Sinclair owns and operates refineries located in Kansas, Oklahoma, New Mexico, Wyoming, Washington and Utah and markets its refined products principally in the Southwest U.S., the Rocky Mountains extending into the Pacific Northwest and in other neighboring Plains states. HF Sinclair supplies high-quality fuels to more than 1,500 branded stations and licenses the use of the Sinclair brand at more than 300 additional locations throughout the country. In addition, subsidiaries of HF Sinclair produce and market base oils and other specialized lubricants in the U.S., Canada and the Netherlands, and export products to more than 80 countries. Through its subsidiaries, HF Sinclair produces renewable diesel at two of its facilities in Wyoming and also at its facility in Artesia, New Mexico. HF Sinclair provides petroleum product and crude oil transportation, terminalling, storage and throughput services to its refineries and the petroleum industry. Equal Opportunity Employer
HF Sinclair Corporation is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status or any other prohibited ground of discrimination.

· The ability to work independently and as part of a team
Skills Required:

· Excellent Communication Skills. Fluency in English and Arabic Language.
· Proficient in personal computing skill.
· Experience with training and study abroad education.
· Time Management

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Position Summary

The Project Coordinator provides administrative and operational support to Project Managers by assisting with project setup, documentation, coordination, and execution activities. This role helps ensure projects move efficiently through internal workflows by supporting communication, maintaining accurate records, and assisting with scheduling, permitting, and invoicing preparation. The Project Coordinator plays a key role in ensuring accuracy, organization, and follow-through while developing foundational skills in project management.

Essential Job Duties

Project Setup & Coordination
· Assist with project setup activities, including surveys, code checks, and documentation collection.
Support permitting and landlord approval processes by gathering required information and tracking status.
Prepare and organize project files and documentation in ERP/project management systems.
Assist Project Managers with developing and maintaining project timelines and schedules.

Design & Approval Support
Submit design requests and track revisions in coordination with the Design team.
Confirm approved designs are documented and ready for Project Manager review before production release.

Production & Installation Support
Assist with releasing projects to production once approvals and documentation are complete.
Help coordinate installation schedules and subcontractor availability.
Support quality control efforts by ensuring installation documentation is complete and accurate.

Financial & Administrative Support
Assist with issuing purchase orders (POs) for installers and materials as directed by the Project Manager.
Support the Project Manager with cost-related closeout activities by gathering required documentation, including final permit costs, freight invoices, and vendor paperwork.
Prepare and compile project closeout documentation to support Project Manager submission to Accounts Receivable for invoicing.
Assist with resolving invoicing discrepancies by gathering supporting documentation and coordinating with internal teams as needed.

Communication & Team Collaboration
Support Project Managers with internal and external communication as needed.
Interact with Estimating, Design, Production, and Service teams as needed to support assigned project tasks.
Document meeting notes, decisions, and follow-up actions as required.
Escalate issues or questions to the Project Manager in a timely manner.

Reporting & Continuous Improvement
Maintain accurate project records and data integrity in ERP systems.
Assist with post-project debriefs and lessons-learned documentation.
Support process improvement initiatives and standardization efforts.
Participate in training and development activities to build project management skills.

Skill and Experience and Education Requirements

Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree preferred, or equivalent work experience.
1–2 years of experience in a project coordination, administrative, or support role preferred.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook).
Strong organizational and documentation skills.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Basic understanding of construction, signage, or service operations is a plus.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Willingness to learn project management processes and systems.

Qualifications

Competency Statement(s)

Attention to Detail – Consistently maintains accurate and complete documentation.
Organization & Time Management – Effectively manages multiple tasks and deadlines.
Communication – Clearly communicates information and follows through on assigned actions.
Collaboration & Teamwork – Supports Project Managers and works cooperatively across departments.
Problem Awareness – Identifies issues early and escalates appropriately.
Adaptability – Adjusts effectively to changing priorities and workloads.
Decision-Making – Exercises sound judgment and makes critical decisions aligned with company procedures and best practices.
Customer Focus – Supports internal and external customer needs with professionalism.
Relationship Building – Develops and maintains positive, productive relationships with clients, colleagues, and stakeholders.
Adaptability & Change Management – Embraces change with a positive attitude, adjusts effectively to shifting priorities, and supports process improvements, system implementations, and organizational growth initiatives.
Safety Awareness – Identifies, communicates, and addresses safety risks to ensure a safe working environment for all employees.

Work Environment / Physical Requirements:

Office-based role with occasional travel within the building or to nearby locations.
Collaborative team environment with opportunities for growth and learning.
Occasional overtime may be required for system upgrades, maintenance, or emergency support.
Ability to walk, stand, and sit for long periods of time.
Ability to lift and carry up to 15 lbs (e.g., moving equipment, hardware installations).

Benefits Offered Full-Time Employees:

No-cost employee benefits for you and low cost for your eligible dependents for competitive medical, dental, vision and prescription benefits.
401K - retirement savings plan with several investment options.
Generous Paid Time Off program that includes company holidays vacation time
Group Rate Life and Disability Insurance.

Work location:
In person in San Antonio

new_jobs.title: Sr. Health & Benefits Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
SGS is the world's leading Testing, Inspection and Certification company. We operate a network of over 2,700 laboratories and business facilities across 119 countries, supported by a team of 99,250 dedicated professionals. With over 145 years of service excellence, we combine the precision and accuracy that define Swiss companies to help organizations achieve the highest standards of quality, safety and compliance.

Our brand promise, when you need to be sure , underscores our commitment to reliability, integrity and trust — enabling businesses to thrive with confidence. We proudly deliver our expert services through the SGS name and trusted specialized brands, including Brightsight, Bluesign, Maine Pointe and Nutrasource.

SGS is publicly traded on the SIX Swiss Exchange under the ticker symbol SGSN (ISIN CH0002497458, Reuters SGSN.S, Bloomberg SGSN:SW).

Job Description

The Sr. Health & Benefits Data Analyst turns complex health and benefits data into clear, actionable insights. This role uses advanced data analytics, multivariate analyses, dashboards, benchmarking, and AI-driven analysis to show how effective programs deliver measurable results, helping position the organization as a leader in benefit analytics. We are looking to hire at least 2 positions.

Job functions:

Data Analysis & Insights

Analyze data related to benefits, including medical claims, pharmacy claims, wellbeing programs, ergonomics, workers’ compensation, disability, flexible work schedule, paid time off, and other benefits. Identify trends, correlations, and leading indicators linking health and wellbeing programs, initiatives, and design to reductions in claims, accidents, and lost-time incidents.
Support descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analyses across benefits programs (e.g., mental health, fertility, medical, ergonomics, occupational health and safety (OHS)).
Enable ongoing evaluation of benefit suppliers and point solutions, including program effectiveness, utilization, outcomes, and cost impact.
Build predictive models to forecast risk exposure, claim frequency/severity, and potential impact of preventive interventions.
Design and deploy AI-driven models (machine learning, NLP, anomaly detection) to uncover hidden patterns in health, safety, and benefits data.
Translate business and research questions into analytic requirements and ensure outputs are accurate, interpretable, and decision‑ready.
Review analytic outputs for quality, validity, and appropriate interpretation before sharing with stakeholders. Provide training and support to benefits managers for data interpretation and validity.
Coordinate with leaders, actuary, and benefit managers to develop and automate recurring reports to ensure timely and scalable insights.

Impact Measurement & ROI Demonstration

Quantify the financial and human impact of safety and wellbeing initiatives.
Develop frameworks to measure return-on-investment (ROI) and value-on-investment (VOI) for health, benefits, and safety programs.
Produce executive-ready narratives that clearly link prevention, wellbeing, and safety investments to business outcomes.
Develop standardized key performance indicators (KPIs) and indices (e.g., incident/utilization rates, claim costs, wellbeing return-on-investment (ROI) metrics) to enable consistent comparisons across industries and geographies.
Use benchmarking insights to translate clear recommendations for program design, investment prioritization, and risk mitigation.

Stakeholder Collaboration

Collaborate closely with a third party analytics vendor and internal health and benefits program managers to design, streamline, and maintain dashboards that support data driven decision-making. Ensure consistent definitions, data quality, and visualization standards across reporting products while translating program needs into clear analytical requirements and insights
Support leadership with data driven insights for strategic decisions related to benefits design, wellbeing initiatives, and safety programs.
Act as a subject matter expert in benefit analytics.
Help manage analytic demand to avoid backlogs, maintain momentum, and ensure sustained analytic capacity.

Governance, Ethics, & Compliance

Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and appropriate use of sensitive health and employee data.
Comply with all relevant data privacy, security, and regulatory requirements.
Support fiduciary responsibilities related to benefits decision‑making by ensuring analyses are rigorous, unbiased, and well‑documented.

Key Deliverables

Executive dashboards and reports linking health, wellbeing, and OHS programs to measurable outcomes.
Industry benchmarking reports with clear performance positioning.
Predictive risk and claims models.
ROI, evaluations, and impact assessments for safety, health, and benefits initiatives.
Data-driven recommendations for continuous improvement.

Qualifications

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Economics, Public Health, Healthcare Economics, Occupational Health, Engineering, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in data analytics, preferably within public health, insurance, benefits, risk management, or occupational health in a large, complex organization.
Strong experience with data analysis tools (e.g., Python, R, SQL, etc) and experience designing and establishing dashboards from raw data using tools such as Power BI
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain complex analyses to non‑technical audiences.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience working with health insurance, wellbeing, disability, or benefits programs or datasets, especially for large employers.
Experience demonstrating program evaluations, outcome measurements, and ROI of health, wellbeing, or safety programs.
Experience working as a liaison between groups to help reach decisions
PhD or PhDc in Data Science, Statistics, Economics, Public Health, Occupational Health, Engineering, or a related field.
Experience supporting executive‑level or senior leadership decision‑making.
Experience with AI/ML techniques for analysis or to generate program insights

Compensation

The expected salary range for this position is $115,000 – $175,000 annually. This range represents the minimum and maximum base salary we reasonably expect to pay for this role. Actual compensation within the range will depend on skills, experience, and qualifications.

Our Benefits

We care about your total well-being and will support you with the following, subject to your location and role.

Health:
Medical, dental and vision insurance, life insurance, employee assistance programs.
Wealth:
In addition to base pay, we offer 401(k) with company match (immediate vesting upon enrollment).
Happiness:
Professional Growth:
Online training courses, virtual and classroom development experiences, tuition reimbursement program
Work-Life Balance:
Paid-time off and family leave

In compliance with applicable state and local pay transparency laws, we provide clear and equitable compensation information for all applicants.

Additional Information

SGS is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and as such we recruit, hire, train, and promote persons in all job classifications without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, age, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetics, status as a protected veteran, or any other characteristics protected by law.

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily with or without reasonable accommodations. The requirements listed above are representative of the knowledge, skills, and/or abilities required.

This job description should not be construed as an exhaustive statement of duties, responsibilities or requirements, but a general description of the job. Nothing contained herein restricts the company’s rights to assign or reassign duties and responsibilities to this job at any time.

If you are applying for a position within the United States and you have difficulty completing the on-line employment application because of a disability, please call 201-508-3149 for assistance and leave a message. You will receive a call back. Please note, this phone number is not for general employment information, but is only for individuals who are experiencing difficulty applying for a position due to a disability.

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
Jobright is a next-generation AI job search platform built to make career navigation faster, smarter, and more personal. They are looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw data into actionable intelligence that shapes how our agents learn, adapt, and perform.

Why Join Us

Work directly on AI systems that are actively changing how people find careers
Your analysis will influence real product decisions, not just sit in dashboards
Collaborate across product, engineering, and client teams in a high-ownership environment
Be part of a team that takes data seriously as a driver of agent improvement

Responsibilities

Embed directly into client-facing and internal workflows, ensuring data-driven insights are woven into the decision-making processes of our AI agents
Design and deliver custom data reports and visualizations that surface clear, expert-level findings for both product teams and external stakeholders
Field data requests with speed and precision, translating complex analytical outputs into accessible, well-communicated narratives
Collaborate regularly with your manager and cross-functional teammates to continuously refine data models and track agent performance over time

Qualifications

Required

Recent grad or early-career professional with 0–2 years of experience in data analysis or a related technical field
Strong communicator with the ability to present data trends clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Solid grounding in statistics, analytical methodologies, and the underlying logic of AI and machine learning systems
Must be based in and authorized to work in the United States; occasional travel for team summits may be required

Preferred

Prior internship or project experience in a data analyst, research, or support role within a tech or AI-focused organization
Demonstrated ability to extract practical, digestible insights from large and complex datasets
Technical proficiency in Python or R, data modeling concepts, and SQL-based database management

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
As a global medtech company, we are driven by our Vision of changing the trajectory of lives for a new day and our Mission to create ingenious solutions that ignite patient turnarounds. Our relentless commitment to patients and strong legacy of innovation in healthcare are the foundation of our future. If you're looking for a new chance, a new beginning, a new trajectory, LivaNova is where your talent can truly thrive. Join our talented team members worldwide to become a pioneer of tomorrow—because at LivaNova, we don’t just treat conditions — we aspire to alter the course of lives.

The Data Analyst will provide data analysis, reporting, and visualization support to the Statistics & Data Science team across post-market surveillance, manufacturing, R&D, and clinical data sources. The Data Analyst will accomplish this by developing efficient, repeatable analytical solutions by automating routine reports, maintaining dashboards, and improving data workflows in collaboration with statisticians and business stakeholders. Additionally, they will translate stakeholder needs into practical data deliverables, with guidance, while ensuring outputs are accurate, validated, documented, and aligned with internal procedures.

Essential Job Functions

Generate routine and ad hoc descriptive analyses, summaries, tables, graphs, and reports to support operational, quality, product, and clinical decision-making.
Develop, automate, and maintain recurring reports and analytical workflows using programming tools and reproducible code to improve efficiency, consistency, and timeliness.
Build, enhance, and maintain dashboards and other data visualization tools that enable stakeholders to monitor key metrics and trends.
Transform stakeholder questions into clear analytical requirements by clarifying objectives, defining deliverables, and confirming timelines, assumptions, and data needs.
Partner with statisticians and subject matter experts to determine when a request is appropriate for a data analyst solution versus when deeper methodological or statistical expertise is required.
Perform data extraction, data cleaning, data preparation, quality checks, and documentation activities to support reliable analyses and reporting.
Support validation and code review activities for analytical programs and reports in accordance with internal procedures, documentation practices, and quality expectations.
Identify opportunities to streamline existing reporting processes, standardize recurring deliverables, and implement practical process improvements that reduce manual effort.
Communicate results clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders through effective written, visual, and verbal summaries, and manage multiple requests/projects with appropriate organization and follow-through.

Minimum Requirements

0-3 years of professional experience in a comparable role supporting data analytics, preferably within a medical device, pharmaceutical company, or CRO.
Bachelor’s degree with excellent track record in mathematics, statistics, data science, computer science, business analytics or equivalent (master’s preferable).
Strong programming skills in R or Python, with experience in both. Advantageous to be familiar with SQL, Power BI, SAS, VBA, JavaScript.
Understanding of mathematical and statistical principles.
Willingness and ability to develop knowledge of device functionality.
Ability to effectively collaborate and communicate cross-functionally with engineers and clinical or medical staff.

Travel Requirements

Occasional travel may be required.

Pay Transparency

A reasonable estimate of the annual base salary for this position is $70,000 - $100,000 plus discretionary annual bonus. Pay ranges may vary by location and will be awarded based on experience.

Employee Benefits Include

Health benefits – Medical, Dental, Vision
Personal and Vacation Time
Retirement & Savings Plan (401K)
Employee Stock Purchase Plan
Training & Education Assistance
Bonus Referral Program
Service Awards
Employee Recognition Program
Flexible Work Schedules

Welcome to impact. Welcome to innovation. Welcome to your new life.

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
About The Company

My3Tech is a leading technology solutions provider dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable IT services to a diverse range of clients. With a focus on cutting-edge technology and customer-centric approaches, we strive to empower businesses by providing tailored solutions that enhance efficiency, security, and growth. Our team of highly skilled professionals is committed to maintaining the highest standards of quality and excellence, ensuring that our clients stay ahead in an ever-evolving digital landscape.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented IT Project Manager to join our dynamic team at My3Tech. In this role, you will be responsible for planning, executing, and overseeing various technology projects from initiation to completion. The ideal candidate will possess excellent leadership skills, a strong technical background, and the ability to coordinate cross-functional teams effectively. You will serve as the primary point of contact for clients and internal stakeholders, ensuring project deliverables are met on time, within scope, and within budget. This position offers an exciting opportunity to work on innovative projects that make a tangible impact on our clients' businesses.

Qualifications

The ideal candidate should have a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. A minimum of 5 years of experience in project management within the IT industry is required. Certifications such as PMP, PRINCE2, or Agile Scrum are highly desirable. Strong understanding of software development lifecycle, infrastructure, and cybersecurity is essential. Excellent communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills are a must. Experience working with cross-functional teams and managing multiple projects simultaneously is preferred. A proactive attitude and the ability to adapt to changing priorities are key attributes for success in this role.

Responsibilities

Develop comprehensive project plans, including scope, objectives, timelines, and resource allocation to ensure successful project delivery.

Coordinate internal resources and third-party vendors for the flawless execution of projects.

Monitor project progress and make adjustments as needed to ensure timely completion.

Communicate project status, issues, and risks effectively to stakeholders and senior management.

Ensure all projects adhere to quality standards and compliance requirements.

Identify and mitigate potential project risks through proactive planning and problem resolution.

Facilitate regular project meetings to track progress and address any challenges.

Maintain comprehensive documentation related to project activities, deliverables, and lessons learned.

Support continuous improvement initiatives by analyzing project outcomes and implementing best practices.

Assist in resource planning and capacity management to optimize project execution.

Benefits

My3Tech offers a competitive salary package aligned with industry standards, along with performance-based incentives. We provide comprehensive health insurance coverage, including medical, dental, and vision plans, to support the well-being of our employees and their families. Our company also offers generous paid time off, including holidays, vacation, and personal days, to promote work-life balance. Employees have access to ongoing professional development opportunities, including training programs, certifications, and workshops, to foster career growth. Additionally, we promote a collaborative and inclusive work environment that values diversity, innovation, and teamwork. Our employees enjoy flexible working arrangements to accommodate their personal and professional needs, along with various employee engagement activities throughout the year.

Equal Opportunity

My3Tech is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe in fostering a diverse workforce that reflects the communities we serve and are dedicated to providing equal employment opportunities to all qualified candidates. Our commitment to diversity and inclusion is integral to our company culture and success.

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst - Procurement
new_jobs.description:
Job description:

Basic Function
Supports the procurement organization’s goals and objectives by leveraging advanced analytical techniques and tools to transform raw data into strategic insights that enhance decision-making and drive operational success. Manage complex datasets, develop sophisticated analytics solutions, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, optimize processes, and implement impactful improvements. Ensures data accuracy and integrity while communicating findings effectively to diverse stakeholders, influencing organizational strategies and fostering data-driven innovation. Job Duties
ESSENTIAL JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: (functions considered essential as defined by ADA)
Data Collection & Management:
Gather, organize, and manage complex datasets from various internal and external sources.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity through rigorous audits, validation, and quality control measures.
Design and maintain advanced data models to support seamless data processing and automated reporting.

Data Analysis & Insights:
Conduct sophisticated analyses using statistical, predictive, and machine learning techniques to solve complex problems and drive business value.
Identify trends, correlations, and opportunities through deep-dive data exploration and advanced modeling.
Develop actionable recommendations to inform strategies and improve business outcomes.

Reporting & Visualization:
Design and implement dynamic dashboards, reports, and visualizations to clearly communicate complex insights.
Tailor presentations to diverse audiences, ensuring both technical and non-technical stakeholders can understand and utilize insights effectively.
Provide proactive and timely responses to ad-hoc reporting needs.

Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement:
Partner with cross-functional teams and leadership to understand their data needs and provide expert-level analytical support.
Act as a key liaison between business units and technical teams to ensure alignment on project goals, metrics, and deliverables.
Mentor and train team members on data tools, systems, and best practices to elevate organizational analytics capabilities.

Process Improvement & Innovation:
Identify and lead initiatives to improve data collection, storage, and analysis.
Develop strategies to automate workflows, enhance efficiency, and support scalable growth.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects that leverage data to innovate and create new business opportunities.

Data Quality & Governance:
Establish and uphold best practices for data governance, ensuring compliance with organizational policies and standards.
Oversee data cleansing, enrichment, and transformation processes to ensure high-quality, reliable datasets.
Advocate for and support policies that promote data integrity and effective data management.

Leadership & Special Projects:
Drive high-impact projects that align with organizational goals and deliver measurable outcomes.
Provide guidance based on data-driven insights to influence decision-making at the leadership level.
Foster a culture of continuous improvement and innovation.
Lead or contribute to special assignments or organizational initiatives as required.

Special assignments or tasks assigned to the employee by their supervisor, as determined from time to time in their sole and complete discretion.
Experience
A minimum of 0- 2 years related experience is required.

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:
Refinery experience and/or experience in procurement, accounting, and inventory management are preferred.
Education Level
A minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree, in a technical or business related field. Each year of experience over five in procurement or inventory control may be substituted for one year of education (nine years total experience required w/o education).
Required Skills
Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize analyze and disseminate significant quantities of information with attention to detail and accuracy; knowledge of the procurement / contracting business and ability to apply this knowledge in providing supporting analytics and reporting; Advanced reading and writing skills; strong background and extensive knowledge of Excel (focus on Power Query/Power Pivot for data transformations); Power BI, SAP tables and QuickViewer query builder, SAP scripting and Microsoft VBA to automate routine manual tasks; familiarity with SQL; proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite; experience with requirements gathering and process documentation; ability to analyze and reconcile data across multiple data sets and systems, interpret data into insights, and develop actionable recommendations; experience with manual bulk data processing using reports/queries and uploads; experience in data analysis methods and using a variety of tools to develop visualizations; business process analysis and change management; extracting data from tables, building queries in SAP; preparing and using large data sets to create dashboards.;strong communication skills to present data-driven insights to senior leadership.
Supervisory/Managerial Responsibility
May have direct reports (contractor and/or HF Sinclair staff).
Work Conditions
Office based with occasional refinery work and with up to 25% travel required by land or air. May include petroleum refinery, warehouse/plant environment, and/or out-of-doors environment.
Benefits
HF Sinclair offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support the well-being of our employees and their families. Our benefits include, but are not limited to, the following: Medical Insurance
Vision Insurance
Dental Insurance
Paid Time-Off
401(k) Retirement Plan with match
Educational Reimbursement
Parental Bonding Time
Employee Discounts

We are committed to fostering a supportive and inclusive work environment, ensuring our employees have the resources needed to thrive professionally and personally. Benefit eligibility is governed by official plan documents, for more details visit Total Rewards. Physical Requirements
Job conditions require, walking, sitting, twisting, stooping, crouching, kneeling, talking or hearing, making precise hand and finger movements, and perceiving color differences, the ability to wear personal protective equipment (beards not permitted), and the ability to operate and drive all assigned company vehicles at company standard insurance rates is essential, valid State driver’s license and proof of insurance required.  Job conditions may require reaching or grasping, lifting and/or carrying up to 20lbs, pushing and/or pulling up to 20 lbs, and climbing up to 15ft. 
Our One HF Sinclair Culture:
At HF Sinclair, we are united through our One HF Sinclair Culture, which is underpinned by our five core values of Safety, Integrity, Teamwork, Ownership and Inclusion. Developed to empower our people, our five core cultural values are at the heart of everything we do and extend to how we engage our stakeholders. These values influence our decisions, shape our behaviors and keep us connected across the entire organization. We maintain a true Safety culture for our employees, communities, environments and customers. Our goal is to make sure everyone returns home safely each day. We have a long-standing commitment to Integrity and ethical behavior and do what is right for our employees, investors, communities and the environment. We encourage employees to Step Up and Stand Out by championing a culture of Teamwork and Ownership. We foster a culture of Inclusion by encouraging diversity of experiences, viewpoints and backgrounds. What makes each of us different, together makes us stronger. About HF Sinclair Corporation
HF Sinclair Corporation, headquartered in Dallas, Texas, is an independent energy company that produces and markets high-value light products such as gasoline, diesel fuel, jet fuel, renewable diesel and other specialty products. HF Sinclair owns and operates refineries located in Kansas, Oklahoma, New Mexico, Wyoming, Washington and Utah and markets its refined products principally in the Southwest U.S., the Rocky Mountains extending into the Pacific Northwest and in other neighboring Plains states. HF Sinclair supplies high-quality fuels to more than 1,500 branded stations and licenses the use of the Sinclair brand at more than 300 additional locations throughout the country. In addition, subsidiaries of HF Sinclair produce and market base oils and other specialized lubricants in the U.S., Canada and the Netherlands, and export products to more than 80 countries. Through its subsidiaries, HF Sinclair produces renewable diesel at two of its facilities in Wyoming and also at its facility in Artesia, New Mexico. HF Sinclair provides petroleum product and crude oil transportation, terminalling, storage and throughput services to its refineries and the petroleum industry. Equal Opportunity Employer
HF Sinclair Corporation is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status or any other prohibited ground of discrimination.

new_jobs.description:
· Maintain accurate and complete student records as required by the management, complete evaluations, analyze results and provide purposeful feedback.

· Evaluate and manage staff performance.
· Maintain a safe and secure working environment.
· Handle student inquiries and complaints.
· Lead generation and lead assignment to team members.
· Develop and maintaining excellent relations with potential students through school visit or social media.

· Conduct individual and group counseling sessions to advise and assist students with academic and vocational development.
· Help students select the right university or programs for their needs.
· Able to work under target pressure and willing to communicate with students.
· Guiding students on university/college application/admission procedures.

new_jobs.title: Project Coordinator
new_jobs.description:
Position Summary

The Project Coordinator provides administrative and operational support to Project Managers by assisting with project setup, documentation, coordination, and execution activities. This role helps ensure projects move efficiently through internal workflows by supporting communication, maintaining accurate records, and assisting with scheduling, permitting, and invoicing preparation. The Project Coordinator plays a key role in ensuring accuracy, organization, and follow-through while developing foundational skills in project management.

Essential Job Duties

Project Setup & Coordination
· Assist with project setup activities, including surveys, code checks, and documentation collection.
Support permitting and landlord approval processes by gathering required information and tracking status.
Prepare and organize project files and documentation in ERP/project management systems.
Assist Project Managers with developing and maintaining project timelines and schedules.

Design & Approval Support
Submit design requests and track revisions in coordination with the Design team.
Confirm approved designs are documented and ready for Project Manager review before production release.

Production & Installation Support
Assist with releasing projects to production once approvals and documentation are complete.
Help coordinate installation schedules and subcontractor availability.
Support quality control efforts by ensuring installation documentation is complete and accurate.

Financial & Administrative Support
Assist with issuing purchase orders (POs) for installers and materials as directed by the Project Manager.
Support the Project Manager with cost-related closeout activities by gathering required documentation, including final permit costs, freight invoices, and vendor paperwork.
Prepare and compile project closeout documentation to support Project Manager submission to Accounts Receivable for invoicing.
Assist with resolving invoicing discrepancies by gathering supporting documentation and coordinating with internal teams as needed.

Communication & Team Collaboration
Support Project Managers with internal and external communication as needed.
Interact with Estimating, Design, Production, and Service teams as needed to support assigned project tasks.
Document meeting notes, decisions, and follow-up actions as required.
Escalate issues or questions to the Project Manager in a timely manner.

Reporting & Continuous Improvement
Maintain accurate project records and data integrity in ERP systems.
Assist with post-project debriefs and lessons-learned documentation.
Support process improvement initiatives and standardization efforts.
Participate in training and development activities to build project management skills.

Skill and Experience and Education Requirements

Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree preferred, or equivalent work experience.
1–2 years of experience in a project coordination, administrative, or support role preferred.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook).
Strong organizational and documentation skills.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Basic understanding of construction, signage, or service operations is a plus.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Willingness to learn project management processes and systems.

Qualifications

Competency Statement(s)

Attention to Detail – Consistently maintains accurate and complete documentation.
Organization & Time Management – Effectively manages multiple tasks and deadlines.
Communication – Clearly communicates information and follows through on assigned actions.
Collaboration & Teamwork – Supports Project Managers and works cooperatively across departments.
Problem Awareness – Identifies issues early and escalates appropriately.
Adaptability – Adjusts effectively to changing priorities and workloads.
Decision-Making – Exercises sound judgment and makes critical decisions aligned with company procedures and best practices.
Customer Focus – Supports internal and external customer needs with professionalism.
Relationship Building – Develops and maintains positive, productive relationships with clients, colleagues, and stakeholders.
Adaptability & Change Management – Embraces change with a positive attitude, adjusts effectively to shifting priorities, and supports process improvements, system implementations, and organizational growth initiatives.
Safety Awareness – Identifies, communicates, and addresses safety risks to ensure a safe working environment for all employees.

Work Environment / Physical Requirements:

Office-based role with occasional travel within the building or to nearby locations.
Collaborative team environment with opportunities for growth and learning.
Occasional overtime may be required for system upgrades, maintenance, or emergency support.
Ability to walk, stand, and sit for long periods of time.
Ability to lift and carry up to 15 lbs (e.g., moving equipment, hardware installations).

Benefits Offered Full-Time Employees:

No-cost employee benefits for you and low cost for your eligible dependents for competitive medical, dental, vision and prescription benefits.
401K - retirement savings plan with several investment options.
Generous Paid Time Off program that includes company holidays vacation time
Group Rate Life and Disability Insurance.

Work location:
In person in San Antonio
